tool_input.summary: No LROC (NAC/WAC) image datasets for the Moon/Marius Hills Pit were returned by this PDS catalog search. The most relevant available PDS datasets for assessing pit depth/geometry and possible subsurface void/cavity indicators at Marius Hills are LRO LOLA (topography), LRO Mini-RF (radar backscatter/CPR mosaics + bistatic), and LRO Diviner (thermal products). Both PDS3 volumes and PDS4 bundles/collections are listed where available.

candidates.title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter LOLA Processed Data (RDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: LOLA processed products (including gridded/topographic RDRs) support measuring Marius Hills Pit depth, rim-to-floor slopes, and surrounding topography relevant to assessing whether a skylight could connect to a larger void.

candidates.title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter LOLA RDR Gridded Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Direct access to LOLA gridded elevation products for local DEM extraction and cross-sections across the pit/skylight area.

candidates.title: LRO-L-LOLA-3-RDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 counterpart to the LOLA processed (RDR) products; useful if your workflow/tools expect legacy PDS3 volumes.

candidates.title: LRO Mini-RF Global Mosaic Archive Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Mini-RF monostatic global mosaics (derived) can be used to examine radar backscatter/roughness context over Marius Hills; sometimes used to assess blockiness and radar-dark anomalies that may correlate with pyroclastics or void-related terrain.

candidates.title: LRO-L-MRFLRO-5-GLOBAL-MOSAIC-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 volume version of Mini-RF global mosaic products for radar-context analysis over the pit region.

candidates.title: LRO Mini-RF Bistatic Archive Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Bistatic Mini-RF observations/derived products are used in some studies to look at scattering behavior that can help interpret near-surface properties; provides complementary constraints to imagery/topography when discussing possible void/cavity presence.

candidates.title: LRO-L-MRFLRO-2_3-5-BISTATIC-V3.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 bistatic Mini-RF archive; legacy equivalent to the PDS4 bistatic bundle.

candidates.title: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Diviner Derived Data Bundle 1
candidates.reasoning: Diviner derived thermal products can help identify anomalous thermal behavior (e.g., colder daytime/warmer nighttime) that may indicate high thermal inertia rockiness or sheltered/cavity-related environments near skylights.

candidates.title: LRO-L-DLRE-5-GDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Diviner gridded data records (GDR) counterpart for thermal mapping in the Marius Hills region.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
What does the bottom of the Marius Hills Pit look like and is there potential for cavity access?

parts.args: {"summary":"No LROC (NAC/WAC) image datasets for the Moon/Marius Hills Pit were returned by this PDS catalog search. The most relevant available PDS datasets for assessing pit depth/geometry and possible subsurface void/cavity indicators at Marius Hills are LRO LOLA (topography), LRO Mini-RF (radar backscatter/CPR mosaics + bistatic), and LRO Diviner (thermal products). Both PDS3 volumes and PDS4 bundles/collections are listed where available.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:lro_lola_rdr::1.0","path":null,"title":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter LOLA Processed Data (RDR) Bundle","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"LOLA processed products (including gridded/topographic RDRs) support measuring Marius Hills Pit depth, rim-to-floor slopes, and surrounding topography relevant to assessing whether a skylight could connect to a larger void."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:lro_lola_rdr:data_gridded::1.0","path":null,"title":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter LOLA RDR Gridded Data Collection","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Direct access to LOLA gridded elevation products for local DEM extraction and cross-sections across the pit/skylight area."},{"dataset_id":"LRO-L-LOLA-3-RDR-V1.0","path":null,"title":"LRO-L-LOLA-3-RDR-V1.0","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 counterpart to the LOLA processed (RDR) products; useful if your workflow/tools expect legacy PDS3 volumes."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:lro_minirf_globalmosaic::1.0","path":null,"title":"LRO Mini-RF Global Mosaic Archive Bundle","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Mini-RF monostatic global mosaics (derived) can be used to examine radar backscatter/roughness context over Marius Hills; sometimes used to assess blockiness and radar-dark anomalies that may correlate with pyroclastics or void-related terrain."},{"dataset_id":"LRO-L-MRFLRO-5-GLOBAL-MOSAIC-V1.0","path":null,"title":"LRO-L-MRFLRO-5-GLOBAL-MOSAIC-V1.0","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 volume version of Mini-RF global mosaic products for radar-context analysis over the pit region."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:lro_minirf_bistatic::5.0","path":null,"title":"LRO Mini-RF Bistatic Archive Bundle","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Bistatic Mini-RF observations/derived products are used in some studies to look at scattering behavior that can help interpret near-surface properties; provides complementary constraints to imagery/topography when discussing possible void/cavity presence."},{"dataset_id":"LRO-L-MRFLRO-2_3-5-BISTATIC-V3.0","path":null,"title":"LRO-L-MRFLRO-2_3-5-BISTATIC-V3.0","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 bistatic Mini-RF archive; legacy equivalent to the PDS4 bistatic bundle."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:lro_diviner_derived1::5.0","path":null,"title":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Diviner Derived Data Bundle 1","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Diviner derived thermal products can help identify anomalous thermal behavior (e.g., colder daytime/warmer nighttime) that may indicate high thermal inertia rockiness or sheltered/cavity-related environments near skylights."},{"dataset_id":"LRO-L-DLRE-5-GDR-V1.0","path":null,"title":"LRO-L-DLRE-5-GDR-V1.0","mission":"Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 Diviner gridded data records (GDR) counterpart for thermal mapping in the Marius Hills region."}]}
